user.title: Set up HEMS
user.description:
HEMS automatically distributes your PV surplus to hot water, home battery and flexible loads.

**How it works:** the meter at the grid connection reports whether you are currently exporting (surplus) or importing power. Combined with the PV forecast, battery state and your priorities, this drives the recommendation and, later, the control.

The setup then guides you step by step through your devices: PV forecasts, storage, hot water, plus switchable and modulated loads. Every step can be skipped and every entry can be edited at any time later via **Configure**.

data_description.meter_entity: Sensor with the current power at the grid connection point in watts. Expected: positive = import, negative = export.
data_description.invert_meter: Enable if your meter reports export as a positive value.
data_description.pv_power_entity: Sensor with the current total PV production across all planes. Used for the load flow and to refine the recommendation.
data_description.invert_pv_power: Enable if your inverter reports PV power with a flipped sign (negative = production).
data_description.pv_minus_battery: Enable if PV and battery are measured at the same point (hybrid inverter), so the PV reading contains the battery power. HEMS then subtracts the battery power from the measured PV (discharge lowers it, charge raises it).
data_description.weather_entity: Weather entity whose daily forecast feeds the planning: on an overcast next day HEMS fully charges the battery instead of covering only the night.
data_description.baseline_load_w: Typical continuous consumption of the house during the day without large consumers (fridge, network, standby).
data_description.night_load_w: Initial value for night-time consumption. HEMS learns the real value automatically from meter statistics after a few days.
data_description.priority_mode: Where should surplus go first? "Automatic" fills the battery first when yield is scarce so the night is covered; with plenty of yield the EV may charge first. Hot-water base heating always has top priority.
data_description.free_capacity_kwh: The "Capacity free" binary sensor turns on when this much energy is available over the given duration without touching the reserve or the night coverage.
data_description.free_capacity_h: Duration over which the energy demand must be covered. PV surplus expected within this window counts towards the free capacity.

add_forecast.title: PV forecast plane
add_forecast.description: A **PV forecast plane** provides the expected solar production, e.g. from the Forecast.Solar or Solcast integration. Add multiple planes (east/west roof) separately — HEMS sums them automatically.

add_storage.title: Storage
add_storage.description: A **storage** (home battery) buffers PV surplus for the night. HEMS derives a target state of charge from night consumption and reserve and schedules charging within the solar window.

data.name: Name
data.soc_set_entity: Target SoC entity (soc_set, device-side charge cap, optional)

data_description.charge_setpoint_entity: Number entity HEMS can use to set the current charging power.
data_description.discharge_setpoint_entity: Number entity HEMS can use to set the current discharge power (e.g. “discharge at 500 W now”).
data_description.reserve_soc: The battery should not be discharged below this level (emergency reserve).
data_description.max_charge_w: Charging power limit; determines how quickly the battery fills within the solar window.
data_description.max_discharge_w: Limit of the discharge power; the battery cannot deliver more than this to the house.
data_description.cold_reserve: This storage joins discharging only when the average SoC of the other storages drops below the reserve threshold (with hysteresis). It always participates in charging, proportionally to its free capacity.

add_thermal.title: Hot water storage
add_thermal.description: A **hot water storage** stores surplus as heat. The base target is always maintained — from the grid if necessary. Heating up to the comfort target happens only on PV surplus.

data.name: Name
data.boost_soc_on: PV boost: battery SoC to start (%)
data.boost_soc_off: PV boost: battery SoC to stop (%)
data.boost_saldo_on_w: PV boost: grid balance to start (W)
data.boost_saldo_off_w: PV boost: grid balance to stop (W)

data_description.base_target: This temperature is always maintained, from the grid if necessary.
data_description.comfort_target: Heating up to this temperature only happens on PV surplus.
data_description.block_start: Start of the daily period in which no hot water is prepared. Leave both empty for no blocking.
data_description.block_end: End of the blocked period. An end before the start means the window runs over midnight (e.g. 18:00 to 06:00).
data_description.legionella_weekday: Weekly hygiene window: on this day the target is raised to the legionella target regardless of surplus — from the grid if necessary. "Disabled" turns the feature off.
data_description.legionella_start: Local start time of the weekly legionella window.
data_description.legionella_end: End of the window. An end before the start runs over midnight.
data_description.legionella_target: Target temperature held during the legionella window.
data_description.boost_soc_on: Heating to the comfort target is only recommended once the total storage SoC reaches this level.
data_description.boost_soc_off: The boost recommendation ends when the SoC falls below this level (hysteresis).
data_description.boost_saldo_on_w: Grid balance that must be reached to start the boost; negative = export (e.g. -2800 = exporting 2.8 kW).
data_description.boost_saldo_off_w: Grid balance at which the boost ends (positive = import).

add_switchable.title: Switchable load
add_switchable.description: **Switchable (sheddable) loads** are devices HEMS can only turn on or off — their power cannot be modulated. Examples: heat pump (SG-Ready/blocking contact), heating rod, pool pump. Minimum runtime and minimum pause protect the device from frequent cycling.

data_description.min_on_min: Once switched on, the device stays on for at least this long.
data_description.min_off_min: After switching off, the device stays off for at least this long.
data_description.max_block_min: The device is never blocked for longer than this per day.
data_description.priority: 1 = highest priority. When surplus is scarce, loads with higher priority are served first.
data_description.heat_coupled: Only for loads whose consumption follows the outdoor temperature (heat pump, heating rod). Only those feed the heating-degree-hour model used for demand forecasting. Surplus-driven loads such as a pool pump or dehumidifier stay off.

add_modulated.title: Modulated load
add_modulated.description: **Modulated loads** can adjust their power consumption continuously. The typical example is a wallbox whose charging current (in amperes) can be regulated between a minimum and maximum. HEMS continuously tracks the setpoint to the available surplus.

data_description.current_entity: Number entity used to set the current setpoint.
data_description.switch_entity: Optional switch HEMS can use to turn the device on and off entirely (e.g. wallbox charging enable).
data_description.min_on_min: Once started, the device runs for at least this long before HEMS stops it (e.g. charge the EV for at least 10 minutes).
data_description.min_a: The device cannot operate below this current (wallbox minimum is usually 6 A).
data_description.priority: 1 = highest priority. When surplus is scarce, loads with higher priority are served first.

forecast_menu.title: PV forecast planes (step 2 of 7)
forecast_menu.description:
A **PV forecast plane** provides the expected solar production, e.g. from the Forecast.Solar or Solcast integration. Add multiple planes (east/west roof) separately — HEMS sums them automatically.

Added so far: {count}

storage_menu.title: Storage / battery (step 3 of 7)
storage_menu.description:
A **storage** (home battery) buffers PV surplus for the night. HEMS derives a target state of charge from night consumption and reserve and schedules charging within the solar window.

Added so far: {count}

thermal_menu.title: Hot water (step 4 of 7)
thermal_menu.description:
A **hot water storage** stores surplus as heat. The base target is always maintained — from the grid if necessary. Heating up to the comfort target happens only on PV surplus.

Added so far: {count}

heating_menu.title: Heating circuit (step 5 of 7)
heating_menu.description:
A **heating circuit** (e.g. heat pump) gets a mode recommendation (heat/cool/off) from outdoor-temperature thresholds with hysteresis, plus a weather-compensated flow temperature from the heating curve. Room heat demand raises the curve; without demand the flow drops to the minimum (setback). During the summer lock months heating is never recommended.

Added so far: {count}

switchable_menu.title: Switchable loads (step 6 of 7)
switchable_menu.description:
**Switchable (sheddable) loads** are devices HEMS can only turn on or off — their power cannot be modulated. Examples: heat pump (SG-Ready/blocking contact), heating rod, pool pump. Minimum runtime and minimum pause protect the device from frequent cycling.

Added so far: {count}

modulated_menu.title: Modulated loads (step 7 of 7)
modulated_menu.description:
**Modulated loads** can adjust their power consumption continuously. The typical example is a wallbox whose charging current (in amperes) can be regulated between a minimum and maximum. HEMS continuously tracks the setpoint to the available surplus.

Added so far: {count}

add_heating.title: Heating circuit
add_heating.description: A **heating circuit** (e.g. heat pump) gets a mode recommendation (heat/cool/off) from outdoor-temperature thresholds with hysteresis, plus a weather-compensated flow temperature from the heating curve. Room heat demand raises the curve; without demand the flow drops to the minimum (setback). During the summer lock months heating is never recommended.

data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Temperature sensor that drives the mode decision and the heating curve.
data_description.demand_entity: Sensor with the room heat demand in percent (e.g. PID thermostat output, several rooms combined via a template sensor). Raises the flow target by up to 5 K; below 1 % demand the flow drops to the minimum (setback).
data_description.heat_on_c: Heating is recommended once the outdoor temperature falls to this value.
data_description.heat_off_c: Heating ends once the outdoor temperature rises to this value (hysteresis).
data_description.cool_on_c: Cooling is recommended once the outdoor temperature rises to this value.
data_description.cool_off_c: Cooling ends once the outdoor temperature falls to this value (hysteresis).
data_description.frost_on_c: Frost protection forces heating once the outdoor temperature falls to this value — even during the summer lock.
data_description.frost_off_c: Frost protection ends once the outdoor temperature rises to this value (hysteresis to prevent cycling).
data_description.heat_lock_from_month: During the lock months (inclusive) heating is only forced by frost protection, otherwise never recommended (summer lock).
data_description.heat_lock_to_month: Last month (inclusive) of the summer lock. A start after the end wraps around new year.
data_description.curve_base_c: Flow temperature target at 0 °C outdoor temperature.
data_description.curve_slope: Reduction of the flow target per degree of outdoor temperature.
data_description.vlt_min_c: The flow target never falls below this value while heating.
data_description.vlt_min_cold_c: Minimum flow target when it is colder than 5 °C outside.
data_description.vlt_max_c: The flow target never exceeds this value.

init.title: Configure HEMS
init.description: Add, edit or remove devices, and change base values and priorities. Every entry can be adjusted at any time.

edit_device.title: Edit device
edit_device.description: Select the entry you want to change. All values can be adjusted afterwards.

edit_switchable.title: Edit switchable load
edit_switchable.description: Adjust settings for "{name}". Switchable loads only know on/off; the timings protect the device from frequent cycling.

data_description.min_on_min: Once switched on, the device stays on for at least this long.
data_description.min_off_min: After switching off, the device stays off for at least this long.
data_description.max_block_min: The device is never blocked for longer than this per day.
data_description.priority: 1 = highest priority. When surplus is scarce, loads with higher priority are served first.
data_description.heat_coupled: Only for loads whose consumption follows the outdoor temperature (heat pump, heating rod). Only those feed the heating-degree-hour model used for demand forecasting. Surplus-driven loads such as a pool pump or dehumidifier stay off.

edit_modulated.title: Edit modulated load
edit_modulated.description: Adjust settings for "{name}". Modulated loads regulate their power continuously via the current setpoint.

data_description.current_entity: Number entity used to set the current setpoint.
data_description.switch_entity: Optional switch HEMS can use to turn the device on and off entirely (e.g. wallbox charging enable).
data_description.min_on_min: Once started, the device runs for at least this long before HEMS stops it (e.g. charge the EV for at least 10 minutes).
data_description.min_a: The device cannot operate below this current (wallbox minimum is usually 6 A).
data_description.priority: 1 = highest priority. When surplus is scarce, loads with higher priority are served first.

remove_device.title: Remove device
remove_device.description: The selected entry is removed from the configuration. The real device is not affected.

general.title: Base values & priorities
general.description: These values were set during onboarding and can be changed here at any time.

Room heat demand raises the curve; without demand the flow drops to the minimum (setback). During the summer lock months heating is never recommended.

data_description.heat_off_c: Heating ends once the outdoor temperature rises to this value (hysteresis).

abort.device_not_found: The selected device was not found.

options.auto: Automatic – battery first when yield is scarce (recommended)
options.battery_first: Battery first – always secure the night
